steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank must provide a subject and verb to introduce the quoted phrase within the relative clause. The subject consists of a common noun (“critic”) followed by a proper noun (“Stina Chyn”) that identifies the specific person.

steps.title: Apply the apposition punctuation rule
steps.content:
A restrictive appositive—one essential to identifying the noun—takes no commas, while a non-restrictive appositive—providing extra information—takes commas.

Option C correctly uses no commas for “critic Stina Chyn”, properly treating the name as a restrictive identifier that specifies which critic.

Options A and B incorrectly use non-restrictive commas; option D violates comma placement rules with unbalanced punctuation.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
